QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_73

question.text: In a composite issue (public issue + rights issue together), how is the promoter contribution calculated?

answer.text: 20% of the total issue size including rights component
20% of the proposed issue or 20% of post-issue capital, excluding the rights component
25% of the public issue component only
20% of the rights issue component only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In a composite issue, the promoter contribution is calculated as 20% of the proposed issue or 20% of the post-issue capital, but the rights component is excluded from this calculation. This is because rights shares go to existing shareholders proportionally. **The Trap:** Including the rights component in the calculation (first option) is the most common mistake. The rights component is excluded because existing shareholders (including promoters) get their proportional rights entitlement separately. **Cross-Exam Context:** Composite issues are a specialized topic in NISM Series IX that combines understanding of both public and rights issue norms. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** In composite issues, always strip out the rights component first before applying the 20% promoter contribution formula — the rights shares are a separate entitlement.
